title: Yuis Ultimate Fate split
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Feb 01, 2012 2:31 am Clover wrote: Question: Has there ever been an eva fic where post EoE Yui travels for eternity before landing on an inhabited planet after the universe itself resets and becoming an Eldritch god through her core? It'd be awesome to see what she was thinking after spending aeons pondering her decisions, and very satisfying to see someone finally destroy her.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 01, 2012 2:49 am EoE's ending doesn't seem to do an especially good job of reconciling Yui's ultimate fate with ... everything else she said and desired. That leaves a big, empty feeling in the pit of my stomach that I've filled with my own personal writings over the years. Of course, all of these entail Yui eventually scraping against the cold vastness of space with life, becoming a mom again, and eventually producing a successor who will repeat the process. Inevitably, much has been sacrificed, and much has been left behind and lost... But the notion of keeping the torch of life burning, in defiance of the universe's cold indifference... Well, that's a considerably brighter approach to the future than floating through oblivion and marinating in loneliness.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Feb 01, 2012 2:59 am Reichu wrote: EoE's ending doesn't seem to do an especially good job of reconciling Yui's ultimate fate with ... everything else she said and desired. That leaves a big, empty feeling in the pit of my stomach that I've filled with my own personal writings over the years. Of course, all of these entail Yui eventually scraping against the cold vastness of space with life, becoming a mom again, and eventually producing a successor who will repeat the process. Inevitably, much has been sacrificed, and much has been left behind and lost... But the notion of keeping the torch of life burning, in defiance of the universe's cold indifference... Well, that's a considerably brighter approach to the future than floating through oblivion and marinating in loneliness.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Feb 01, 2012 3:03 am Bagheera wrote: It does make her whole "plan" seem a lot more sensible, yes. As described it's . . . a little batty. It would have made more sense, IMO, to run with the plan as described but ultimately stick the soul of Lilith in Unit 01 and send it on her way while Yui is reunited with Gendo at the end after aborting the whole Instrumentality gig. I can see them coming up with such a plan, and I can see Gendo doggedly pursuing it while he keeps Yui locked up in Unit 01. But what we got, willingly abandoning your family and your people for an eternity of loneliness . . . yeesh.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Feb 01, 2012 3:12 am Here's what I sent xanderkh, for anyone who's interested. The scene per se is embedded in a post I never made on EGF: . . . For lo these past many years I've been writing a fic, and this segment just sort of came to me in a vision while thinking about something completely different, and I just had to show it to somebody. This is something that I figured should be included in the ending. All you need to know is that the fic -- called The Rest of Evangelion -- is a sort of extended director's-cut of ep17 onward which adds new scenes and expands existing scenes to present a bigger picture of what's happening, both explaining existing subplots and adding new ones for kicks. A new tenth of the iceberg revealed, if you will -- some made up, others representing (roughly) what I really do think is happening behind the scenes. There was nothing to see out there, anymore -- nothing to feel, hear, or sense in any way. The universe was at its limit. Soon, now, it would all be over: the end of everything. Even she was going to end, because there would be nothing and nowhere for her to exist anymore. "So this is what it all came to," her dream-self within the Eva said. "It all returns to nothing anyway..." Her body was covered in the little nicks and imperfections she had acquired -- or rather, decided to keep -- in the billions, trillions, and quadrillions of years of her existence since Earth. They were the wounds of accidents, and of wars. Some were from wars that had been started against her. Others were from wars she had started. And some were wounds caused by her own spear -- self-inflicted, to create mouths that could talk to her through pain. Billions, trillions, and quadrillions of years after the sun, the moon, and the Earth were gone, all that was left was her... and now even she was going to cease to exist, for existence itself, it seemed, was coming to an end. And only now, at the end of everything, could she finally ... First it was labored breathing as the feelings welled up within her chest. Then it was facial contortions as they beat against her face from the inside. Then she cupped her hands to her face as they came pouring out through her eyes, and she sobbed loudly into her hands. Finally, she could cry. Because even now, at the end of everything, at the end of a chasm of billions, trillions, and quadrillions of years, all she could think about was that brief flicker that had occurred before it all, that tiny infinitesmal amount of time when she had been happy. When she had been truly alive. She couldn't even really remember anymore what had happened since then. There were others, and she had communicated, and she had fought, and she had been hurt and hurt others. She had overseen the flourishing of new life and the destruction of others. But she remembered her life clearly. The warm arms of her husband... The smile of her child... Over time her humanity had been stripped away, but the memory of it remained clear. A humanity and life that she had abandoned for the sake of this ... existence. An existence whose only purpose was to prove itself. But there was no one left to prove anything to. Now she realized: She had made a mistake. 'Meaning' had long since left her. Doing things for what they 'mean' or 'prove' meant nothing to her anymore. There was no one left to lie to: She had chosen to run away from life, in favor of merely existing. And now it was too late to take it back... Then, a flicker. A realization. A realization that was delivered to her from across time. It was a message from herself, a set of memories that suddenly appeared in her mind: the her at the end of her life merging with the her at the end of her existence, a gift of her past self from her future self to her present self. Memories billions, trillions, and quadrillions of years ago, from a past self who had chosen differently, whom in that quantum flicker she could see emerging at the shore from a giant, red sea. She closed her eyes, smiling, and was finally at peace.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 01, 2012 3:25 am Bagheera wrote: It would have made more sense, IMO, to run with the plan as described but (...)

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Feb 01, 2012 6:56 am Reichu wrote: That's not very poetic, though. The beauty of the scenario I propose is a "cyclicity of life" thing, where the torch is passed from one generation to the next. (Meaning, yes, sacrifices must be made along the way -- but if you think the cosmos is a better place with life in it, then it's for the better cosmic good, even if it might generate a microcosm of pain here and there.) Humans create Seeds create humans create Seeds, ad infinitum, as long as the universe allows. The Earth and Sun are going to die. So what? Do what the FAR did, and start a new biosphere with a new humanity at a new star system. Eva-01 is Lilith's successor in every way.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 01, 2012 7:52 am Bagheera wrote: Well, yes, but why does Yui have to be involved?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 01, 2012 9:08 am @Monk Ed http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Graphical_timeline_of_the_Stelliferous_Era

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Feb 01, 2012 1:23 pm I don't really see any contradiction between Yui's optimism/attitude and the monument thing. In fact, only someone who strongly thinks humanity/life etc are really worth it would make such a sacrifice/ decided that what it all being remembered is worth facing the reality of being all alone for all of eternity Doesn't the thought that everything we know will someday be forgotten/there will be no one to remember any of it seem horrible to you? If you knew you're about the only one who can prevent that, wouldn't you do it? Wouldn't you feel like it's your duty? I mean, when not a single molecule that used to be part of Shinji or Gendo will be intact, Yui's memories of them will still be there. While real people will always be first-and-foremost, people, Characters in a work will occassionally be born from abstract concepts like "the big adult"; Perhaps ppl feel so much of a need to deconstruct yui to bits because the other characters, while still rich in archetypic substance, have lots of tangible human elements to them, but yeah, that's why the other characters appear all the time and yui gets only so and so much little sequences... She doesn't have that much dimension. But real ppl are influenced by concepts like that, so we have a character like her to be part of Shinji's and Gendo's background. Sometimes a character has to be perfect for other characters or the plot to work; A character like Yui wouldn't work if she was the protagonist and we got shown her poV, but she needs to be the way she is to hold the meaning she does. (Note that Shinji parts with her in the end) Also, you don't have to be perfect to think that humanity is worth such a sacrifice... also, after EVA 01 has both the "fruits", what applies to beings like Shinji (that they can't live meaningfully on their own) doesn't have to apply to her. She is a goddess in a way, or a representation of the same concept that the idea of gods springs from...

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Feb 01, 2012 4:53 pm Bagheera wrote: @Monk Ed: You might want to adjust your timeline a little. Current theories are that star formation will cease in the high billions or perhaps trillions of years from now, but not much more beyond that.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Feb 01, 2012 5:06 pm Monk Ed wrote: ...And where in the story do I appear to err in regards to that?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Feb 01, 2012 5:44 pm Bagheera wrote: If she's having this breakdown at all it will be after the stars have died out, which will be at best trillions of years down the road, not quadrillions.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 01, 2012 6:07 pm trillions, quadrillions, of years bah. Terayears, petayears, yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Feb 01, 2012 7:02 pm Monk Ed wrote: Regardless: I'm getting much too defensive, all you said was "you might" and here I am going off like this.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Wed Feb 01, 2012 10:04 pm I always figured an all-enduring monument like that would result in a universal contraction. I mean, after expansion slows down enough for matter to be subject to local gravity -- after near-total heat death.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Feb 01, 2012 10:18 pm arkiel wrote: I always figured an all-enduring monument like that would result in a universal contraction. I mean, after expansion slows down enough for matter to be subject to local gravity -- after near-total heat death.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Feb 01, 2012 10:34 pm Everything on every conceivable scale is ultimately pointless unless you find meaning in it for yourself, and even then you're just willfully fooling yourself. But hey, if that's all there is then we might as well enjoy it, no? Cheerful nihilism for everyone.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Feb 01, 2012 10:45 pm NemZ wrote: Everything on every conceivable scale is ultimately pointless unless you find meaning in it for yourself, and even then you're just willfully fooling yourself. But hey, if that's all there is then we might as well enjoy it, no? Cheerful nihilism for everyone.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 01, 2012 11:01 pm Bagheera wrote: If Reichu's right and she actually can create life (the whole ark thing)

It'd be awesome to see what she was thinking after spending aeons pondering her decisions, and very satisfying to see someone finally destroy her.
